column1: # Pre-release PROMPT eval — golden set (TAB-separated; '#' lines ignored).
# columns:  question <TAB> must_contain <TAB> must_not_contain <TAB> manifest
#   must_contain / must_not_contain are COMMA-separated literal substrings checked
#   against the generated program (grep -F). This is the STABLE tier: it asserts the
#   model picks the right TOOLS / program SHAPE, which is far less flaky than scoring
#   the numeric answer. Each rule encodes a privacy_box-system.md HARD RULE or a
#   regression we fixed (the "$224,303 phone bill"). Shapes checked:
#     transactions(  — spending/count/total/biggest use the structured tool
#     money(         — amounts formatted with money(), not String(x)
#     manifest(      — vault-structure questions answer from the manifest
#     .tags / <tag>  — category spending filters transactions() on a KNOWN tag
#                      (the tag name appears in the filter), NOT ask_local/search
#     SUGGEST_TAG    — an UNKNOWN durable category MUST propose a tag
#                      (# SUGGEST_TAG: name : q) — required, not optional — including
#                      recurring bills/utilities (electricity/gas/water/internet); it
#                      may still classify inline this time; and must NOT force a wrong
#                      near-match (e.g. "health" for gym/electricity)
#     ask_local      — reading a value out of a DOCUMENT (a renewal date, a plate)
#                      calls the on-device reader; a meta count must NOT call it at
#                      all. An EXTRACTION/reading query stays INLINE and must NOT emit
#                      a `# SUGGEST_TAG:` (over-tag guard) — tags live on TRANSACTION
#                      .desc, not on documents. `ask_local_batch` is the DEFAULT for a
#                      LIST; single ask_local is the one-item case.
#     months_ago(    — a relative-date question ("last N months/days", "this year")
#                      uses the wall-clock API (wall_clock()/months_ago()/days_ago())
#                      and filters t.date >= … ; a row requiring it ALSO trips a
#                      scoped guard if the program hardcodes a YYYY-MM-DD literal
#                      (relative windows come from the clock, never a baked-in date).
#     ].id           — manifest files are VaultFile (field is `.alias`, not `.id`;
#                      `files[i].id` won't compile — the phone-bill regression)
#     search(        — wrong for exact transaction aggregates / meta questions
#     $" +           — manual dollar concat = the raw-float formatting bug
#     kpi( / table( / series( — RICH OUTPUT (Phase 2): emit typed result data. A
#                      total/count → kpi; a per-month/day breakdown → series; a
#                      ranked list → table. The CLIENT picks the chart, not the model.
#     money_val(     — typed money in result data (raw + $ display); the spec-money
#                      lint FAILS a bare float or a money() string handed to a builder
#                      (incl. a geo_map .place(code, money()) — .place is guarded too).
#     map(           — a GEO breakdown (spending by country/state) emits a geo_map(…)
#                      block, grouping on Txn.country (ISO3) / Txn.state (US 2-letter).
#                      NB the builder is geo_map( (map is a Mojo prelude name) — the
#                      literal "map(" still matches as a substring of geo_map(.
#     .merchant      — top-merchants groups on the cleaned index-time .merchant field,
#                      NOT raw .desc (store numbers/cities fragment one merchant).
# The fixture tags (eval/fixtures/data/categories.txt) are the product defaults:
# phone, travel, restaurant, groceries, health, transfers, rewards. gym is deliberately
# NOT one → SUGGEST.
# question
how much did I pay for my phone bill
how much did I spend on groceries
how much did I spend on gym membership
how many transactions do I have
what is the total of my transactions
what was my biggest transaction
how many documents are in my vault
when does my car insurance renew
show my spending by month
give me a dashboard of my total spending and income
list my top merchants by total spending
which countries did I spend the most in
what are my expenses in the last 3 months
how much did I spend on electricity over the last 6 months
what is the license plate on my car
